title: CharacterSheet
description: Canonical, versioned contract for a complete character sheet. Contract-first: this document is the source of truth; the generator, validator and decoder are made to conform to it. Structural only — game vocabulary (ability/skill/class/species/spell/background/resource/condition names) is catalog-driven and never hard-coded here, so this file is content-neutral.

schema_version.description: Contract version. Bump on any breaking change to this schema.

meta.description: Non-sheet provenance. Optional; ignored by the validator.

lineage.description: Lineage sub-choice identifier (catalog). Present only for species that offer lineages (e.g. elf lineages, tiefling fiendish legacies). Null when the species has no lineages.

species_variant.description: Species-variant option identifier (catalog). Used for per-species variant axes like draconic ancestry. Null when not applicable.

size.description: Size identifier (catalog); species-set, sometimes a per-character choice.

background.description: Background identifier (catalog); required — it grants the ability-score increases and the origin feat.

abilities.description: One entry per ability. Keys are ability identifiers from the catalog (not enumerated here to stay content-neutral).

saving_throws.description: One entry per ability, keyed by ability identifier (catalog).

skills.description: One entry per skill, keyed by skill identifier (catalog).

passive_scores.description: Passive scores keyed by skill identifier (catalog) → value (10 + modifier, + situational bonuses). Includes at least perception.

source.description: What grants this effect (item name, class feature, etc.).

permanent_effects.description: Permanent effects granted by items, features, or other sources. Each entry carries provenance so the sheet shows WHY a stat changed. The mechanical impact is validated against the grant spine; this field is for display and provenance tracking.

armor_class.description: The current effective AC — the authoritative number used in play.

armor_class_detail.description: How armor_class was derived, so it can be re-computed when armour or an ability changes. Optional; armor_class stays authoritative. armor_class = base + dex_bonus + sum(bonuses[].value).

source.description: Catalog identifier for the AC formula in use (worn armour / an unarmoured-defence feature / a spell).

base.description: Flat base before the Dexterity contribution and any bonuses.

dex_bonus.description: Dexterity contribution actually applied (after any armour cap).

bonuses.description: Every additional AC contribution, each tagged with what grants it, so the total sums while provenance is retained. A shield, a Ring of Protection, a Shield of Faith, etc. are each one entry.

source.description: What grants it — an item / spell / feature identifier (catalog) or short label.

initiative.description: Initiative modifier added to the d20 (Dexterity modifier plus any bonuses); not the '10 + mod' passive score.

speed.description: Movement speeds keyed by mode identifier (catalog) → speed in feet. At least the base walking mode is present; special modes (fly/swim/climb/burrow) appear when granted. Authoritative — speed_detail (if present) explains how these were derived.

speed_detail.description: How the speeds map was derived, so it can be re-computed and each grant traced. Optional; the speeds map stays authoritative. base applies to the mode named by base_mode; final(mode) = (base if mode == base_mode else 0) + the sum of that mode's modifiers, where a modifier contributes its value, or (if relative) factor × final(relative.of). Mirrors armor_class_detail.

base.description: Base walking speed before modifiers (from the species).

base_source.description: What grants the base walking speed — the species identifier (catalog).

base_mode.description: The speed-map mode identifier (catalog) that `base` applies to — i.e. which key is the base walking mode. Lets the map be re-derived from this block.

modifiers.description: Every speed grant/adjustment, each tagged with what grants it, so the map sums while provenance is retained. A granted fly/swim/climb/burrow speed is one entry; a bonus to walking speed is another. A modifier is either a fixed `value` (feet) or `relative` to another mode (a speed 'equal to' or 'twice' your walking speed).

source.description: What grants it — a species / feature / feat / item identifier (catalog).

value.description: Fixed signed feet this source contributes to the mode (a granted non-walk speed is its full value). Use this OR relative, not both.

relative.description: A speed defined relative to another mode (e.g. a Climb Speed equal to — or twice — your walking speed). Contributes factor × final(of).

of.description: The mode identifier (catalog) whose final speed this tracks — usually the base walking mode.

factor.description: Multiplier of the referenced mode's speed — 1 for 'equal to', 2 for 'twice'.

max.description: Full Hit Point maximum, before any temporary reduction.

max_reduction.description: Temporary reduction to the maximum (effective max = max − max_reduction); restored to 0 on a long rest.

hit_dice.description: Hit dice by die size. Keys look like 'd8', 'd10'; each value is a {max, remaining} pool — remaining drops as dice are spent on short rests.

heroic_inspiration.description: Whether the character currently holds the rerollable heroic inspiration.

active_concentration.description: The spell the character is currently concentrating on (catalog identifier), or null. At most one at a time.

active_effects.description: Ongoing timed effects that are neither formal conditions nor class resources — buffs, active spell effects on self, temporary bonuses. Live play state; empty at a fresh build.

source.description: What produced it — a spell/feature/item identifier (catalog).

duration.description: Remaining duration, e.g. a rounds/minutes/until-rest identifier.

companions.description: Secondary creatures the character controls — familiars, summoned creatures, animal companions, mounts, or a wild-shape/polymorph form. Empty for most characters.

senses.description: Special senses, keyed by sense identifier (catalog) → range in feet (0 for a non-ranged sense).

defenses.description: Damage and condition defences granted by species, features, feats or items. All lists optional; absent = none. Entries are catalog identifiers (damage type / condition names).

resistances.description: Damage-type identifiers (catalog) the character takes half damage from.

immunities.description: Damage-type identifiers (catalog) the character takes no damage from.

vulnerabilities.description: Damage-type identifiers (catalog) the character takes double damage from.

condition_immunities.description: Condition identifiers (catalog) the character cannot suffer.

save_advantages.description: Advantage on saving throws by ability ID or scope keyword. Scope keywords: concentration, death, spells. Ability IDs from the ability catalog (intelligence, wisdom, charisma, dexterity, etc.).

effect.description: avoid_or_end = advantage on saves to avoid or end the condition. end = advantage only on saves to end it.

condition_advantages.description: Advantage on saving throws related to specific conditions. Each entry specifies the condition and whether the advantage applies to saves to avoid/end it, or only to end it.

attacks.description: Optional weapon/attack rows as shown on the sheet. Derived/for-render; not required.

weapon_masteries.description: Weapon identifiers (catalog) the character currently has the mastery property of. Independent of the (render-only) attacks list.

attunement.description: Magic-item attunement slots. max defaults to 3, raised by some features; attuned = slots currently in use.

equipped.description: Items currently worn/wielded, keyed by slot identifier (catalog) → the item in that slot. Each is a single instance. See item.schema.json.

backpack.description: Carried-but-not-equipped items — mundane and magic — one line each (with a quantity). See item.schema.json.

treasure.description: Coin purse. All denominations optional; absent = 0.

class_resources.description: Per-class expendable resources, keyed by class identifier (catalog) → resource identifier (catalog) → its counts. The set of resources each class has is catalog-defined, not enumerated here.

feats.description: Feats the character has taken. At least one — every character gets an origin feat from its background.

spellcasting.description: Null for a wholly non-casting character. Otherwise the casting block: one or more casting SOURCES (a class, a feat, a species or an item — each with its own spell ability and DC), the shared leveled/pact slot pools, and the spell list. Slots are OPTIONAL: a source can be slotless (species cantrips, an origin feat that grants a spell cast once per long rest without a slot), so a character may cast without any slot table.

sources.description: Every source granting this character spells, keyed by source identifier (catalog) — a casting class, or a feat/species/item. At least one.

spells.description: The character's spells. Unique by (name, source): a spell may appear more than once only under DIFFERENT sources (each cast with that source's ability/DC). The validator enforces this composite-key uniqueness — JSON Schema cannot.

flavour.description: Optional flavour block, populated by the backstory step. NOT part of legality — the validator ignores it.

item.description: The item contract, bound by version. This is the ONLY place the item-schema version is named; equipped/backpack both point here. The target is a URN — a stable identifier resolved offline via the schema registry, never a network fetch.

subclass.description: Subclass identifier, or null until unlocked.

subclass_detail.description: An extra choice that specialises the subclass — e.g. a subclass that offers a further sub-choice (a chosen terrain, element, etc.). Absent when not applicable.

class_detail.description: A class-level choice that shapes the entire class, e.g. a divine order or primal order chosen at level 1 that grants proficiencies. Absent when not applicable.

abilityEntry.description: One ability. base = pre-increase assignment; final includes the background increase plus feat/ASI bumps (max 30). The background increase must not raise base above 20.

base.description: Pre-increase assignment (never above 20 at any level; later boosts land in final, capped at 30).

background_bonus.description: The background's ability-score increase for this ability.

reduction.description: Temporary reduction to final (effective score = final − reduction); restored to 0 on a long rest. modifier reflects the effective score.

source.description: Where the proficiency comes from, or null if not proficient.
Free-text origin, e.g. a class+level, 'Species', or 'Background'.

choices.description: Selected option identifiers (catalog) for a feature that grants a choice — e.g. the specific options a choice-granting feature lets the character pick. Absent/empty for a feature with no choice.

source.description: Where the feat slot came from — e.g. a background-granted feat, an ability-score-improvement slot at a class level, or a high-level boon.

ability_increase.description: If this feat granted an ability-score increase, the ability and amount. Null/absent when it did not.

pool.description: A depletable count with a maximum and how much is currently left (e.g. spell slots at a level, hit dice of a size).

resourceEntry.description: One expendable resource (a limited-use ability's charges or a point pool) — usable for class resources and for a feat/feature's own uses.

recharge.description: Recharge trigger identifier(s) (catalog) — a single trigger, or a list when more than one applies (e.g. a short OR long rest).

recharge_amount.description: How much is regained on recharge — an integer, or a catalog token like an 'all' identifier. Absent = full.

castingSource.description: One source of spells — a class, or a feat/species/item that grants casting — with its own spell ability and derived DC/attack. Slot-based budgets are optional because not every source has them.

kind.description: What the source is (catalog) — a class / subclass / feat / species / lineage / item. A subclass source covers a subclass's always-prepared grants, and a subclass that grants its own spellcasting (carrying its own budgets).

cantrips_known.description: Cantrips this source grants/knows at the current level. Optional — absent when it grants none.

prepared_limit.description: Level-1+ spells this source can prepare (the budget; excludes always-prepared grants). Optional — only a prepared-casting class has a budget; a feat/species grants a fixed set.

slotTable.description: Slots by spell level. Keys are the spell level as a string ('1'..'9'); each value is a {max, remaining} pool — remaining drops as slots are spent and resets on the appropriate rest. Slot tables are optional overall (a slotless caster omits them), but when present a table must record at least one level — an empty table carries no information.

weapon_mastery.description: Weapon-mastery property identifier (catalog), if the wielder has it.

companion.description: A lightweight stat block for a secondary creature — enough to run it in play. The consumer resolves any named ability/trait against the catalog, as with the main character.

kind.description: What sort of companion (catalog) — familiar / summon / animal companion / mount / shape.

source.description: What granted this companion — an item, class feature, feat, or spell. E.g. 'Figurine of Wondrous Power (Silver Raven)'.

duration.description: How long the companion remains. E.g. '12 hours', '1 minute', 'until dismissed'. Absent for permanent companions.

spell.description: One spell the character holds. Three independent axes: source (who grants it → the ability/DC used), bucket (how it is HELD, for counting), and recovery (how it is CAST). Uniqueness is on (name, source) — see the spells array.

source.description: REQUIRED — the casting source this spell comes from; a key in spellcasting.sources, which supplies the ability/DC/attack used to cast it. For a repeatable feat it is an instance identifier (a per-variant id for a feat taken more than once) that resolves to the feat type plus its chosen-list variant. Every spell is attributed.

bucket.description: How the spell is HELD, for counting (independent of how it is cast). 'cantrip' = a chosen cantrip (counts against the source's cantrips_known); 'prepared' = a leveled spell prepared from the source's budget (counts against prepared_limit); 'always' = an always-available grant, any level, ADDITIVE (does not count against a budget) — covers BOTH fixed grants and constrained-choice grants (a chosen spell that must satisfy a school/tag/list constraint), each checked by the validator against the source's grant spec (a fixed list or a choose{from} constraint), not an exact set; 'known' = in a known/spellbook pool but not currently prepared (not castable until prepared).

recovery.description: How the spell is CAST (a slotless spell's rest CADENCE lives in uses.recharge, so cadence has ONE home). The meaning of an absent value is bucket-dependent: a 'cantrip' is cast at will; a 'prepared'/'always' leveled spell is cast via shared spell slots; a 'known' (unprepared spellbook) spell is not currently castable. Explicit values: 'spell_slot'/'pact_slot' = via that slot pool; 'slotless_per_rest' = a slotless cast with its own use pool (uses.recharge names the rest — short or long); 'at_will' = a cantrip; 'ritual_only' = castable only as a ritual. The validator enforces the bucket↔recovery coupling.

uses.description: For a slotless per-rest spell, its own expendable use pool — max casts (1 for a once-per-rest grant, the proficiency bonus for some species grants) and remaining. Absent for at-will and slot-cast spells.

secondary_cast.description: An ADDITIONAL casting avenue for the SAME (name, source) — e.g. a granted spell castable slotless once per rest that is ALSO slot-castable. Convention: the characteristic/limited avenue is the primary `recovery` (usually the slotless-per-rest grant); the fallback avenue goes here (usually `spell_slot`). Only ONE secondary avenue is modelled (no source needs more than two). Records the second avenue without a duplicate spell entry (which would double-count against the source's grant). Absent for the common single-avenue case.
